metadata.title: LayoutTransformer: Layout Generation and Completion with Self-attention

sections.title: Introduction
content: Scene layout generation is crucial for synthesizing realistic scenes in diverse domains such as images, mobile applications, documents, and 3D objects. LayoutTransformer leverages self-attention to model contextual relationships between layout primitives, enabling generation from scratch or completion of partial layouts.

The framework treats layouts as unordered sets of graphical primitives—each with semantic category and geometric attributes (position, scale)—and uses an autoregressive Transformer to predict new elements one parameter at a time. It supports variable-length layouts and captures semantic relationships without explicit supervision.

sections.title: Model Architecture
content: LayoutTransformer represents each layout primitive by factorizing its semantic and geometric attributes into independent latent vectors. Geometric coordinates (x, y, h, w) are discretized via 8-bit quantization to better handle symmetries in structured layouts like documents and UIs.

The model uses a masked Transformer decoder with teacher forcing during training. Each layout sequence starts with a <bos> token and ends with an <eos> token. The architecture predicts the next primitive attribute conditioned on all previously generated tokens, enabling flexible layout generation and completion.

sections.title: Experiments and Results
content: LayoutTransformer is evaluated across four diverse datasets: COCO (natural scenes), PubLayNet (documents), RICO (mobile app wireframes), and PartNet (3D shapes). It achieves competitive performance using the same architecture and hyperparameters across all domains.

Quantitative results show that LayoutTransformer outperforms or matches state-of-the-art methods in metrics like NLL, FID, IS, Coverage, and Overlap. Notably, it generates well-aligned, non-overlapping elements in document and UI layouts, and produces plausible 3D object assemblies.

sections.title: Semantic Understanding and Attention Visualization
content: Despite being trained only for layout generation, LayoutTransformer learns semantic embeddings that cluster related object categories (e.g., cats and dogs) and support word2vec-style analogies (e.g., 'snowboard:snow :: surfboard:sea'). This demonstrates that layout modeling implicitly captures high-level semantic structure.

The self-attention mechanism allows visualization of contextual dependencies: when predicting 'skis', the model attends most to 'snow'; when predicting 'person', it attends to 'skis'. This provides interpretability and confirms the model’s understanding of real-world spatial relationships.
